title: aquilegia formosa var. truncata
intro: Aquilegia formosa var. truncata, also known as the cut-leaved form of the western columbine, is a flowering plant species native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Its health benefits include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as acting as an expectorant to aid in the relief of coughs and colds. Traditionally, the plant was prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with some herbalists also using it in salves and ointments to treat skin conditions.
